_comment: The fake camera's sample-frame catalog (mobile-implementation.md §10; capture-qc.md §1–2, M-10 per-pose QC). Front scenarios render the bundled frontal frame, side scenarios the neutral dark side frame — what varies is the simulated pipeline analysis: `metrics` overrides the passing-frame defaults (capture_qc.dart CaptureFrameMetrics), and MeasurementRepositoryFake runs the REAL per-pose capture-qc.md threshold tables over them, so each fail code is reproduced by its documented rule (first failure in table order, per pose), never by a hardcoded verdict. Pick a scenario per pose from the capture screen's dev-flavor QC selector. NOTE: the front passing defaults FAIL the side table by design (a frontal subject is exactly what `not_side_profile` rejects) — side scenarios must override the orientation + arms metrics.

samples.label: Pass — frontal, well lit
QC fail — undecodable image
QC fail — low resolution (short edge 320px < 480px)
QC fail — poor lighting (mean luma 24 outside [40, 215])
QC fail — blurry (Laplacian variance 32 < 60)
QC fail — no body detected
QC fail — two bodies in frame
QC fail — partial body (key visibility 0.31 < 0.5)
QC fail — side pose (shoulder/hip ratio 0.72 outside [0.9, 2.2])
QC fail — tilted phone (24° > 15°)
QC fail — arms fused to torso (clearance 2% < 5%)
QC fail — too far (body 28% of frame < 40%)
Multi-fault — dark + blurry + far (reports poor_lighting only)
Pass — right profile, arms relaxed
QC fail — not side-on (frontal shoulders: z-delta 0.04 < 0.15)
QC fail — arms not relaxed (clearance 8% > 5%)
QC fail — poor lighting on the side frame (mean luma 24)
QC fail — blurry side frame (Laplacian variance 32 < 60)
QC fail — too far on the side frame (body 28% < 40%)
Side multi-fault — frontal + arms out + far (reports not_side_profile only)
